latest.title: Koalas don't hug trees for shade
latest.script_key: they hug for life those cute paws lock in place for hours why their fur s insulation fails at 86 f so they press into the trunk to cool down that s why ever seen one let go on its own

latest.title: Birds keep their eggs warm with a trick you missed
latest.script_key: that bird isn t just sitting there watch the feathers around her belly puff up like a blanket those aren t fluffy decorations they re skin touching the eggs pumping warm blood straight to them because birds don t have belly buttons like we do that patch is basically a built in heating pad

latest.title: Sharks can feel electricity in the water
latest.script_key: sharks can feel electricity in the water watch the head before the turn because sharks have sensory pores that detect tiny electric fields from other animals that helps them hunt when sight is limited the ocean is full of signals we cannot feel would you swim differently

latest.title: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next

latest.title: Auroras glow when solar particles hit air
latest.script_key: auroras start with charged particles from the sun watch the moving color because charged particles follow earth s magnetic field toward the upper atmosphere when they hit oxygen and nitrogen the air releases light the sky is reacting to space weather which rare sky event next

latest.title: Elephants cool blood through giant ears
latest.script_key: elephants use their ears as living radiators watch the ear surface because warm blood flows through those broad veins moving air can dump heat before it spreads through the body that is why the ears are more than decoration in hot places which giant animal trick next

latest.title: Rock layers store ancient environments in stripes
latest.script_key: rock layers are time stamps made of stone watch the stripe pattern because each layer can mark a different setting river mud ocean floor windblown sand or volcanic ash stack enough layers and the cliff becomes a timeline which rock clue should we read next

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal weather from above the clouds
latest.script_key: storm clouds look different when seen from above watch the cloud tops because the upper shape shows where air is rising flattening or spreading out from above weather becomes a texture map instead of a ceiling which above cloud clue should we compare next

latest.title: Coral reefs are built by tiny living builders
latest.script_key: coral reefs are built by tiny living builders watch the reef texture because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next

latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: The moon keeps one face turned toward Earth
latest.script_key: the moon shows us one face for a reason watch the moon s position because its rotation and orbit are locked together it spins once in about the same time it circles earth so the same side keeps facing us that is synchronized motion not stillness which orbit clue next

latest.title: Sheep recognize faces across the flock
latest.script_key: sheep can remember familiar faces watch the face and spacing because sheep can learn who is familiar in the flock that recognition helps them stay near safe partners and avoid stress the group is not just a blur to them which farm animal should we compare next

latest.title: Cave rocks that grow like trees underwater
latest.script_key: cave rocks grow like trees because water drips all year adding minerals bit by bit you can tell the difference instantly the ones hanging down are stalactites the ones standing up are stalagmites that s why the tallest one in this cave is older than you d guess did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Whales send songs across huge ocean distances
latest.script_key: whales can send sound farther than sight watch the body rise and listen to the idea behind it because whale sound can travel through water far better than light songs and calls help with contact over huge distances the ocean is a sound network which call should come next

latest.title: Feathers on the forest floor aren't just dropped
latest.script_key: this feather isn t lost it s a clue see the tiny brown specks those aren t dirt that s downy fluff from a hawk s belly not a songbird s hawks pluck their own feathers when they re nesting like a pillow refill that s why it s on the forest floor not in a nest did you think only songbirds shed feathers

latest.title: Cats use whiskers like built-in rulers
latest.script_key: cats measure tight spaces with their whiskers watch the whiskers near the face because they sense tiny touches and air changes that helps cats judge gaps hunt in low light and protect their eyes before contact the face is doing math what pet sense should we test next

latest.title: Owls hide where you least expect them
latest.script_key: this owl didn t pick its perch by accident those vertical stripes aren t random they break up its shape against the bark that s why it disappears when it doesn t even move eye twitches just one give it away if you squint so here s the real question how long did you stare before you spotted it

latest.title: Lions use dark ear marks to guide cubs
latest.script_key: lions have dark ear marks cubs can follow watch the backs of the ears because those dark marks can stand out when a lion moves through grass for cubs that contrast helps track the adult ahead the detail is small but the pride depends on staying together would you spot it

latest.title: Dogs read the world through scent maps
latest.script_key: dogs build a map with smell watch the nose before the feet because every sniff samples layers of scent dogs can learn who passed where they moved and what changed since the last check the ground is basically a news feed what should they sniff next

latest.title: Dragonflies see every insect's weak spot
latest.script_key: watch this one twice notice how those eyes bulge like tiny binoculars that s because they see in 360 degrees with built in rangefinders that s why they snatch mosquitoes mid air no blind spots did you know their eyes have 30 000 lenses each
